title: Two-seed RealMLP with one n_ens32 expert
summary: Use n_ens=32 only for the second RealMLP seed while keeping the first seed at the parent ensemble size.
rationale: A full n_ens=32 upgrade for both seeds may be expensive. A mixed ensemble tests whether one stronger or more stable seed is enough to capture most of the benefit while preserving the cheaper original expert.
implementation_hint: Start from the parent two-seed RealMLP average code. Keep the first RealMLP expert unchanged. Change only the second RealMLP expert to n_ens=32, preserve the same folds and features, and average the two probability vectors. Do not add other experts, feature changes, stacking, or calibration.
expected_effect: Improve OOF AUC if a higher-ensemble second expert adds useful stability or diversity at lower cost than upgrading both seeds.
risk: The mixed setup may be less balanced and may not improve over the equal two-seed parent.
